title: armeria pseudarmeria subsp. tejedensis
intro: Armeria pseudarmeria subsp. tejedensis, also known as the Canarian Thrift, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. It is a medicinal plant with properties that have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as colds, sore throats, and skin irritations. Canarian Thrift can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in traditional herbal medicine to treat digestive issues, coughs, and respiratory infections.
